LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E).) AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "INTENTIONAL TORT - MENTAL SUFFERING" THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI ENGAGED IN OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT, OR CONDUCT "SO EXTREME AS TO EXCEED ALL BOUNDS OF THAT USUALLY TOLERATED IN A CIVILIZED SOCIETY." (COCHRAN V. COCHRAN (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 488, 494.) AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT MS. GATTI OWED A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED HOW THE COMPLAINT COULD BE AMENDED TO STATE FACTS ESTABLISHING A DUTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR # 5963, TEL. NO. (707)246-3323 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Protective Order (Discovery)
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harris B. Taback, of Law Offices of Harris B. Taback, Tel. 415.241.1400, for plaintiffs;
Jennifer Naegele, of Law Offices of Jennifer Naegele, Tel. 415.519.9116, for plaintiffs;
Peter W. Daniel, of Viola Law Firm P.C., Tel. 650.343.6400, for defendant Antonio DiNapoli.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PLAINTIFF MAYA DI NAPOLI Motion To Enforce Protective Order is GRANTED and the deemed cross-motion by defendant Antonio Di Napoli to modify the protective order to allow use of Confidential Information in the family law case is DENIED. All Confidential Information as defined in the protective order must be used only for purposes of this case, and not for the family law case, and must be destroyed or retained for preservation/storage purposes as provided for in paragraphs 10 and 11 of that order. However, nothing in this order precludes the parties in the family law case from propounding discovery in that case and, if there is any dispute about such discovery, those disputes must be resolved in the family law case, not this case. At the time the parties' counsel signed the stipulated protective order on February 18, 2016 they knew that there was a pending family law case between Ms. Di Napoli and Mr. Di Napoli and nonetheless they agreed to a protective order limiting the use of Confidential Information as defined in the order to this case only subject to modification by the parties' agreement or court order. The parties have not agreed to any modification of the protective order to allow the use of Confidential Information in the family law case and at no time until after this case was settled and this motion was filed did Mr. Di Napoli seek a court order modifying the protective order to allow for the use of Confidential Information in the family law case. While the effect of granting this motion and denying the deemed cross-motion may be to make certain otherwise relevant and admissible information in the family law case unavailable for use in that case, that was clearly within the parties' contemplation and there has been no showing that the protective order was procured by fraud, duress or mistake or is otherwise unenforceable by reason of public policy, illegality or other grounds. The protective order is thus an enforceable contract between the parties and there is no reason now to disturb the parties' agreement or the terms of that order based on grounds that were easily foreseeable at the time the protective order was signed by the parties' counsel. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF MAYA DI NAPOLI'S Notice Motion To File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED on the following three conditions: 1) if Mr. Di Napoli wishes a trial continuance, Ms. Di Napoli will not oppose a trial continuance of six months or less; 2) Ms. Di Napoli must make herself available for an additional deposition not to exceed three hours limited to questions relating to the new claims; and 3) Ms. Di Napoli must serve verified responses without objections within ten days to the following deemed-served discovery: a) state all facts supporting each of your new claims; b) identify each person with knowledge of any of those facts and state what facts that person knows; and c) identify all documents relating to those facts and produce them to the extent such document have not been produced. Granting the motion is strongly favored by California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. With the three conditions, there is no prejudice to Mr. Di Napoli by the granting of the motion. The court cannot determine as a matter of law that any of the new claims are time-barred. To the extent that it has not been fully covered by the prior IME, a new IME is permitted as to the new claims only. The parties must meet and confer on this, and if there is disagreement, may come back on an ex parte basis and show where it has been previously covered. The burden will be on plaintiff counsel to show the portions of the transcript that has been covered. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. COLLEN DUFFY-SMITH OF ATTY. GEOFF BENTZEL OF MORGAN DUFFY SMITH & TIAGLO LLP, TEL. NO. (408)244-4570 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PETER W. DANIEL OF VIOLA LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 343-6400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT
ANA GATTI;
ATTY. MEGAN NOTTINGHAM OF CROSBY & ROWELL LLP, TEL. NO. (510)267-0300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FOUR SEASON HOTELS LTD.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI'S DEMURRER TO the 4th and 5th causes of action in the Complaint is sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the fourth cause of action for "intentional tort - mental suffering" the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts establishing that Defendant Anna Gatti engaged in outrageous conduct, or conduct "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized society." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 494.) As to the fifth cause of action for negligence, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not plead facts establishing that Ms. Gatti owed a duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not demonstrated how the complaint could be amended to state facts establishing a duty. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963, Tel. No. (707)246-3323
